purpose: This repository implements a hooks service for the CRB.Cos.Lending.Selling domain that publishes notifications about loan selling-related events. It acts as an integration point that converts domain events into standardized notifications that can be consumed by other systems via a hooks pattern.

This repository defines data contracts for a loan selling and management system in the lending domain. It contains type definitions for loan entities, purchase processes, interest calculations, and account management, serving as a shared contract library between different services.
features: Loan selling and purchasing between financial institutions
ambiguities: The exact services or applications consuming these contracts are not specified

Purpose of the 'field1' through 'field21' properties on LoanInfo
Implementation details of the 'GroomingRecovery' process
Whether PII data is encrypted or specially handled beyond the attribute marking

purpose: COS.Lending.Selling.UI is a React-based frontend application for Cross River Bank's loan selling platform. It provides interfaces for loan management, tracking loan history, and facilitating loan transactions between different stakeholders in the lending ecosystem.
features: LTHF (Loans To Be Held For Sale) management
ambiguities: The relationship between loans and LTHFs (Loans To Be Held For Sale) is not clearly defined in the code
The full authentication flow and token management strategy is not completely visible from the frontend code
The specific business rules for loan approvals and the complete approval workflow are not fully documented in the code
The exact schema of loan entities and their relationships is not fully defined in the frontend code

purpose: This repository provides an AI-powered question-answering system for the CRB Lending/Selling platform, enabling natural language queries about loans, their history, interest calculations, transfers, and fees. It functions as a backend service that connects to a database containing loan information and uses Amazon Bedrock LLM APIs to process questions and generate insights.
features: Natural language querying of loan data
Conversational chat interface with context retention

purpose: This repository contains Airflow DAGs for orchestrating data processing workflows in a lending and selling system. It manages the ingestion, transformation, and curation of loan data, along with handling fee collections, interest accruals, and reporting for financial operations.

This repository serves as the database model and context for the Cos.Lending.Selling system, defining the data schema and relationships for a financial loan selling and servicing platform. It provides entity models, configurations, and migration capabilities to support the persistence layer of the lending system.
ambiguities: The exact mechanism for how tenant filtering is applied to database queries is not clear from the model definitions alone
Integration points with external systems for loan origination are implied but not explicit in the model
The relationship between SellingContext and any API layer is not defined in the available files
Payment processing systems that might integrate with this database model are not clearly identified

purpose: This repository contains utilities for resolving and mapping accounts for loans in a lending and selling system. It queries account information from multiple sources (accounting system, loan operations database) and updates the selling database with the proper account mappings needed for financial operations.
features: Account resolution for loans (purchase, return, interest, fee accounts)
Custom purchase account mapping based on loan details

purpose: cos-lending-selling-datatools is a data engineering repository for the Cross River Bank lending and selling platform. It provides tools for data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) using dbt, Airflow, and Python to facilitate analytics and reporting for the loan selling business.

This repository handles data ingestion from various sources into a lending/selling system. It processes loan data, servicing information, SOFR rates, and provides monitoring metrics through CloudWatch for a lending and loan selling platform.
features: Loan data ingestion from various marketplace lenders (MPLs)
Servicing data import from CSV files in S3
SOFR rate ingestion from Federal Reserve API
Sync/comparison between Arix and Selling databases
ambiguities: The relationship between this ingestion service and a possible orchestration system (DAGs are mentioned but their implementation is not in this repo)
The exact structure of the loan selling flow is not clear from the ingestion code alone

How contract_repo.py is used within the broader system

purpose: The repository serves as a backend WebAPI for loan management in a lending platform, specifically handling the selling and purchasing of loans. It enables MPLs (Marketplace Lenders) to purchase loans and manage loan lifecycle operations including transfers, fees, interest accruals, and investor relationship management.
